questions.question: What is the atomic number of carbon?
questions.explanation: From Section 1: Atomic Numbers and Mass Numbers, the atomic number of carbon is indeed 6.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: From Section 2: Chemical Symbols, the chemical symbol for gold is Au.

questions.question: What is the formula for water?
questions.explanation: From Section 3: Chemical Formulas, the formula for water is indeed H2O.

questions.question: What is the definition of an acid?

options.C: A substance that releases hydrogen ions
options.D: A substance that gains oxygen atoms

questions.explanation: From Section 4: Acids and Bases, the definition of an acid is indeed a substance that releases hydrogen ions.

questions.question: What is the process called when a solid changes directly to a gas?
questions.explanation: From Section 5: States of Matter, the process called when a solid changes directly to a gas is indeed sublimation.

questions.question: What is the unit of measurement for energy?
questions.explanation: From Section 10: Energy and Work, the unit of measurement for energy is indeed Joule (J).

questions.question: What is the law of conservation of mass?

options.A: Mass cannot be created or destroyed in a chemical reaction.
options.B: Energy cannot be created or destroyed in a physical process.
options.C: Matter cannot be created or destroyed in an atomic reaction.
options.D: Substances are either elements or compounds.

questions.explanation: From Section 13: Laws of Thermodynamics, the law of conservation of mass is indeed mass cannot be created or destroyed in a chemical reaction.

questions.question: What is the difference between an element and a compound?

options.A: An element is a substance that consists of only one type of atom, while a compound is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms.
options.B: An element is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms, while a compound is a substance that consists of only one type of atom.
options.C: A compound is a substance that consists of only one type of atom, while an element is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms.
options.D: Elements and compounds are the same thing.

questions.explanation: From Section 16: Elements and Compounds, the difference between an element and a compound is indeed an element is a substance that consists of only one type of atom, while a compound is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms.

questions.question: What is the term for the study of the structure of matter?
questions.explanation: From Section 19: Crystallography, the term for the study of the structure of matter is indeed crystallography.

questions.question: What is the difference between a pure substance and a mixture?

options.A: A pure substance is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms, while a mixture is a substance that consists of only one type of atom.
options.B: A mixture is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms, while a pure substance is a substance that consists of only one type of atom.
options.C: A pure substance is a substance that consists of only one type of atom, while a mixture is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms.
options.D: Pure substances and mixtures are the same thing.

questions.explanation: From Section 22: Pure Substances and Mixtures, the difference between a pure substance and a mixture is indeed a pure substance is a substance that consists of only one type of atom, while a mixture is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms.

questions.question: What is the unit of measurement for temperature?
questions.explanation: From Section 25: Temperature and Thermometry, the unit of measurement for temperature is indeed Kelvin (K).

questions.question: What is the process called when a liquid changes directly to a gas?
questions.explanation: From Section 28: States of Matter, the process called when a liquid changes directly to a gas is indeed boiling.

questions.question: What is the law of conservation of energy?

options.A: Energy cannot be created or destroyed in a physical process.
options.B: Mass cannot be created or destroyed in a chemical reaction.
options.C: Matter cannot be created or destroyed in an atomic reaction.
options.D: Substances are either elements or compounds.

questions.explanation: From Section 31: Laws of Thermodynamics, the law of conservation of energy is indeed energy cannot be created or destroyed in a physical process.

questions.question: What is the term for the study of the behavior of matter?
questions.explanation: From Section 34: Thermodynamics, the term for the study of the behavior of matter is indeed thermodynamics.

questions.question: What is the difference between a homogeneous mixture and a heterogeneous mixture?

options.A: A homogeneous mixture is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms, while a heterogeneous mixture is a substance that consists of only one type of atom.
options.B: A heterogeneous mixture is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms, while a homogeneous mixture is a substance that consists of only one type of atom.
options.C: A homogeneous mixture is a substance that consists of only one type of atom, while a heterogeneous mixture is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms.
options.D: Homogeneous mixtures and heterogeneous mixtures are the same thing.

questions.explanation: From Section 37: Mixtures, the difference between a homogeneous mixture and a heterogeneous mixture is indeed a homogeneous mixture is a substance that consists of only one type of atom, while a heterogeneous mixture is a substance that consists of two or more types of atoms.

questions.question: What is the unit of measurement for volume?
questions.explanation: From Section 40: Units of Measurement, the unit of measurement for volume is indeed liter (L).

questions.question: What is the process called when a solid changes directly to a liquid?
questions.explanation: From Section 43: States of Matter, the process called when a solid changes directly to a liquid is indeed melting.

questions.question: What is the law of conservation of momentum?

options.A: Momentum cannot be created or destroyed in a physical process.
options.B: Energy cannot be created or destroyed in a chemical reaction.
options.C: Mass cannot be created or destroyed in an atomic reaction.
options.D: Substances are either elements or compounds.

questions.explanation: From Section 46: Laws of Motion, the law of conservation of momentum is indeed momentum cannot be created or destroyed in a physical process.

questions.question: What is the term for the study of the motion of objects?
questions.explanation: From Section 49: Kinematics and Dynamics, the term for the study of the motion of objects is indeed dynamics.

questions.question: What is the difference between a force and an energy?

options.A: Force is a push or pull on an object, while energy is the ability to do work.
options.B: Energy is the ability to do work, while force is a push or pull on an object.
options.C: Both force and energy are the same thing.
options.D: Neither force nor energy can be defined.

questions.explanation: From Section 52: Forces and Energy, the difference between a force and an energy is indeed force is a push or pull on an object, while energy is the ability to do work.
